summary: The test suite covers all five public functions with both happy-path and common error scenarios. Get-CurrentVersion, Get-BumpType, Invoke-VersionBump, New-ChangelogEntry, Set-VersionInFile, and the integration orchestrator are all exercised. Key strengths: proper BeforeEach/AfterEach temp-dir isolation, clear Arrange/Act/Assert comments, descriptive It-block names, and correct Pester $script: scoping. Gaps that reduce rigor: no test for empty/null commits array in Get-BumpType or New-ChangelogEntry; scoped commits (feat(scope): message) are never tested even though the implementation supports them; the 'Other Changes' changelog section is set up in one test but never explicitly asserted; the VERSION-file path is absent from integration tests (only package.json is used there); and there is no test confirming that an empty VERSION file throws the expected error. The error-message wildcard patterns (Should -Throw '*not found*') are reasonable but could match too broadly. Overall a well-structured suite that would catch most regressions, but the missing boundary/edge-case tests leave some implementation corners unverified.
